title: Media Industry Report 2025
subtitle: Breaking boundaries across media verticals
description: Comprehensive survey across Gaming, Social Media, Streaming, Publishing, Advertising, and Music. Confidential — authorized access only.

verticals.name: Gaming
verticals.summary: Cloud gaming, monetisation shifts, and the rise of indie studios are reshaping how players spend time and money.

insights: Roblox leads as #1 most played game — 16.5% rank it first, ahead of Fortnite (11.7%)
50% of 13-17 y.o. have played their top game for 2+ years — deep loyalty
Immersive gamers play 7.2 hrs/wk vs 6.3 for non-immersive — 14% more engaged
Gameplay + friends are the top play drivers — graphics rank lower than social factors

blocks.heading: Game variety
blocks.subtitle: Number of different games played in the last year
blocks.question: Q37: In the last year, how many different games have you played?
blocks.takeaway: 25% of gamers play 10+ different games annually

Age group.title: Games played — by age group (%)
Age group.note: Average: 5.8 games (2-12 y.o.) vs 5.6 games (13-17 y.o.)

Primary device.title: Games played — by primary device (#)
Primary device.note: Console gamers skew toward 10+ games (avg 6.3 games).

Gamer type.title: Games played — immersive vs non-immersive (%)
Gamer type.note: Non-immersive gamers slightly more concentrated at 10+ (28% vs 23%).

blocks.heading: Top games
blocks.subtitle: Most frequently played games ranked by preference
blocks.question: Q38: List the 3 games that you currently play most often. Put your most played game first.
blocks.takeaway: Roblox, Fortnite, Minecraft dominate the top 3

Rank distribution.title: Top 10 games — rank distribution (% of respondents)
Rank distribution.note: Roblox leads Rank 1 at 16.5%, Fortnite 11.7%, Minecraft 10.3%.

Device usage.title: Top games — multi-device vs single-device (%)
Device usage.note: Call of Duty highest multi-device (56%); Free Fire mostly single-device (88%).

blocks.heading: Play tenure
blocks.subtitle: How long gamers have been playing their most frequent game
blocks.question: Q39: When did you first start playing [most frequent game]?
blocks.takeaway: Older teens show significantly deeper game loyalty

Age group.title: Play tenure — by age group (%)
Age group.note: 50% of 13-17 y.o. playing 2+ years vs 29% of 2-12 y.o.

By game.title: Top games — tenure breakdown (%)
By game.note: Fortnite highest long-tenure (55% at 2+ yrs). Candy Crush only 15%.

blocks.heading: Play motivation
blocks.subtitle: Why gamers play their most frequent game
blocks.question: Q40: Why do you like to play [most frequent game]? Put 1-3 reasons with the first being most important.
blocks.takeaway: Social factors rival pure gameplay quality

Rank distribution.title: Top reasons — rank distribution (%)
Rank distribution.note: Gameplay is #1 (19% rank first), friends (10%), immersive world (9%).

Gamer type.title: Immersive vs non-immersive — % citing each reason
Gamer type.note: Immersive gamers value personalization (+16pp) and immersive worlds (+15pp).

blocks.heading: Time consumption
blocks.subtitle: Weekly hours spent on most frequent game
blocks.question: Q41: In a typical week, how many hours do you play [most frequent game]?
blocks.takeaway: Immersive gamers play 14% more hours weekly

Age group.title: Weekly hours — by age group (%)
Age group.note: 13-17 y.o. play heavier: 17% at 11+ hrs/week vs 13% of 2-12 y.o.

Gamer type.title: Weekly hours — immersive vs non-immersive (%)
Gamer type.note: Immersive gamers: 49% play 6+ hrs/wk vs 37% non-immersive.

blocks.heading: Device usage
blocks.subtitle: What devices gamers play on
blocks.question: Q42: What devices do you play [most frequent game] on?
blocks.takeaway: PlayStation leads; multi-device gaming is rising

All devices.title: Devices used for most frequent game (% of respondents)
All devices.note: Multi-select (n=894, 1,295 total mentions). Avg 1.4 devices per gamer.

verticals.name: Social Media
verticals.summary: Platform usage, creator economy, algorithm-driven content discovery, and social commerce trends.

verticals.name: Streaming & OTT
verticals.summary: Ad-supported tiers, password-sharing crackdowns, live sports licensing, and content ROI.

verticals.name: Publishing & Print
verticals.summary: Digital-first titles, AI content disruption, audiobook growth, and subscription bundling.

verticals.name: Advertising & Adtech
verticals.summary: Cookie deprecation, retail media networks, CTV growth, and AI-driven creative production.

verticals.name: Music & Audio
verticals.summary: Streaming royalties, AI composition, live event economics, and podcast growth.
